HERO.TITLE: Hi, this is my portfolio website
DESCRIPTION: Frontend is more than just code. It's magic when design and technology work in unison.
I help ideas come to life through modern, beautiful, and responsive interfaces.

INTRO.TITLE: About Me
INTRO.PARAGRAPH1: Hi! My name is Anatoliy, I'm a frontend developer creating modern and responsive web interfaces.
INTRO.PARAGRAPH2: My stack includes HTML, SCSS, JS, TypeScript, and Angular. Tailwind helps me build stylish and adaptive interfaces quickly, and RxJs lets me manage reactivity. I use Firebase and GitHub for data storage and deployment.

FACTS.TITLE: Key Facts
LIST: 🎨 I create user-friendly UI/UX interfaces focused on usability
📱 Responsive design for all devices — from phones to desktops
⚡ I optimize load speed and performance
📚 I write semantic, scalable, and clean code

PORTFOLIO.TITLE: Personal Portfolio
PORTFOLIO.TYPE_DESCRIPTION: A single-page application where all content is loaded without page reload.
PORTFOLIO.DESCRIPTION_SHORT: Multilingual SPA portfolio with advanced animations, filtering, and synchronized content. Built with a focus on UI/UX, custom features, and skill visualization.

DETAILS.ABOUT_PROJECT: A single-page application (SPA) built as an interactive portfolio focused on showcasing UI/UX skills, animations, component synchronization, and Angular architecture. All pages are interconnected through Angular Router, with structured, dynamically rendered data for translations, skills, and projects.
DETAILS.OVERVIEW_PROJECT: The portfolio includes 'About', 'Skills', and 'Projects' sections linked via Angular Router. Each section features custom animations: from staged element transitions to synchronized interactions. It includes a custom slider with autoplay, swipe gestures, keyboard navigation, skill-based project filtering, and dynamic JSON output. The interface is adaptive, multilingual, and built with Angular, Tailwind CSS, and ngx-translate.
DETAILS.SPECIAL: The site showcases both visual and technical skills: custom animations, interactive cards, section transitions, SVG graphics, Angular signals, and skill/project filtering. Content is organized in TypeScript structures and translation files, allowing for easy scalability.
DETAILS.CHALLENGES: Performance and visual polish were key focuses: section tracking, sequential animations, and optimized deferred rendering were implemented. Managing a large number of custom animations required precise synchronization between components and styles.

WOT_BLITZ_STATISTICS.TITLE: WoT Blitz Statistics
WOT_BLITZ_STATISTICS.TYPE_DESCRIPTION: A single-page application where all content is loaded without page reloads.
WOT_BLITZ_STATISTICS.DESCRIPTION_SHORT: A WoT Blitz stats tracker with advanced metrics, session analysis, ratings, and multilingual support.

DETAILS.ABOUT_PROJECT: A commercial web application for analyzing player statistics in the popular tank shooter WoT Blitz.
DETAILS.OVERVIEW_PROJECT: The application is intended for WoT Blitz players aiming to improve their gameplay. It allows searching for players and clans, tracking game statistics, analyzing session results, ratings and progress, and provides quick access to the latest data.
DETAILS.SPECIAL: An intuitive and responsive interface designed in a dark gaming style, focused on usability, fast navigation, and maximum readability of statistical data. The application supports multilingual functionality and is translated into 5 languages, making it accessible to a broad international audience.
DETAILS.CHALLENGES: Data was processed and normalized not only from the official game API but also from third-party sources to obtain the most complete and accurate results. Complex mathematical formulas were used to calculate and analyze game metrics. The project was a serious challenge in terms of handling large data volumes, computational logic, and building complex application state.

WEATHER_FORECAST.TITLE: Weather Forecast
WEATHER_FORECAST.TYPE_DESCRIPTION: A single-page application where all content is loaded without page reloads.
WEATHER_FORECAST.DESCRIPTION_SHORT: Interactive weather app with geolocation, hourly breakdown, and a responsive interface.

DETAILS.ABOUT_PROJECT: A responsive web application for displaying weather forecasts using the OpenWeatherMap API. It provides detailed information about current weather conditions and forecasts for the coming hours and days.
DETAILS.OVERVIEW_PROJECT: The application automatically detects the user's location through geolocation and displays the current weather. The user can also manually enter a city. It provides detailed forecasts with temperature, humidity, visual icons, and hourly breakdowns. The interface is adapted for various devices.
DETAILS.SPECIAL: The responsive interface is built using Flexbox and Grid. Styled icons and graphic elements are used to visually represent the current weather conditions.
DETAILS.CHALLENGES: Multiple APIs were used to obtain a complete picture: data had to be processed, normalized, and aggregated manually. Special attention was given to accurate hourly forecast display. The project served as practice for working with REST APIs, calculations, and interface adaptation for different screens.

JS_SLIDER.TITLE: JS Slider
JS_SLIDER.TYPE_DESCRIPTION: A reusable visual component (e.g., slider or card), designed for embedding in other projects.
JS_SLIDER.DESCRIPTION_SHORT: Custom responsive slider in vanilla JavaScript with swipe, autoplay, and navigation support.

DETAILS.ABOUT_PROJECT: A custom slider implemented in pure JavaScript without using third-party libraries. It is a reusable UI component with flexible configuration.
DETAILS.OVERVIEW_PROJECT: The slider includes keyboard control, swipe support, navigation buttons, and autoplay. It supports responsive layout and is easy to embed in any project. The core logic is split between a config file and a functional module.
DETAILS.SPECIAL: The component is written from scratch in native JavaScript. Two versions were implemented: using constructor functions and classes. Both versions support centralized configuration for easy control. The advanced version includes swipe support. The interface is fully responsive and does not use any frameworks.
DETAILS.CHALLENGES: The project provided solid practice in developing reusable components and building architecture without libraries. Advanced features such as swipes, autoplay, keyboard navigation, and responsiveness were implemented. It also included experience migrating from functions to classes and organizing configuration settings.

GRID_TEMPLATE.TITLE: Grid Template
GRID_TEMPLATE.TYPE_DESCRIPTION: A one-page site or promo page focused on visuals and presentation, usually without logic.
GRID_TEMPLATE.DESCRIPTION_SHORT: Landing page template with responsive CSS Grid layout and mobile hamburger menu.

DETAILS.ABOUT_PROJECT: A landing page template built using CSS Grid without frameworks. The project was created to demonstrate responsive layout and practice building complex grids from scratch.
DETAILS.OVERVIEW_PROJECT: The landing consists of static blocks laid out with CSS Grid. It features responsive navigation, including a jQuery-powered hamburger menu for mobile. Otherwise, the site is fully static and contains no interactive elements.
DETAILS.SPECIAL: The project was implemented without using frameworks. Modern CSS Grid features like grid-template-areas, auto-fit, and minmax were actively used. Responsiveness is achieved using media queries for four breakpoints. The project is bundled using a custom Gulp configuration.
DETAILS.CHALLENGES: Although layout itself wasn’t difficult, the project served as practice in well-thought-out responsiveness and mastering all features of CSS Grid. Special attention was paid to cross-browser compatibility — vendor prefixes were added using Gulp.

CRUD_MATERIAL.TITLE: CRUD with Angular Material
CRUD_MATERIAL.TYPE_DESCRIPTION: An application with create, read, update, and delete operations.
CRUD_MATERIAL.DESCRIPTION_SHORT: Modern Angular 20 CRUD app with Angular Material, role-based access, and Firestore-backed public/sandbox catalogs.

DETAILS.ABOUT_PROJECT: A rewritten demo application for managing an electronics catalog with full CRUD operations. Built with Angular 20, Angular Material, and Firebase Firestore, with separate public and sandbox data zones.
DETAILS.OVERVIEW_PROJECT: The app supports create, read, update, and delete operations through dialogs and reactive forms, plus filtering, sorting, and pagination in a Material table. Users sign in anonymously by default, while admin access is available via Google sign-in with custom claims. Data is synchronized in real time from Firestore collections.
DETAILS.SPECIAL: The project uses modern Angular architecture: standalone components, `inject()` API, signals, and new template control flow (`@if`, `@for`). Access logic is role-based: admins can manage public and sandbox records, regular users can manage only their own sandbox data. UX includes localized paginator labels, snackbars, responsive layout, and custom SCSS organization.
DETAILS.CHALLENGES: The main challenge was designing secure role-based access across frontend logic and Firestore rules while combining public and per-user sandbox data in one interface. Additional focus was on handling asynchronous CRUD flows, clear user feedback, and maintaining a clean component/service structure after the full rewrite.

KINOZAL.TITLE: Kinozal
KINOZAL.TYPE_DESCRIPTION: A ready-made layout or educational template without active logic. Used to showcase layout skills.
KINOZAL.DESCRIPTION_SHORT: Educational cinema site template built with Bootstrap 5, responsive layout, and SCSS theming.

DETAILS.ABOUT_PROJECT: An educational template for an online cinema, implemented as a two-page responsive website. The project was created to practice layout using Bootstrap 5 and SCSS.
DETAILS.OVERVIEW_PROJECT: The template includes login pages, a news section, movie ratings, articles, and a poster gallery. All elements are styled using Bootstrap theming. The interface includes basic interactive elements: navigation and standard dropdown menus.
DETAILS.SPECIAL: The project used Bootstrap 5 theming with enhancements via SCSS: custom variables, mixins, and flexible style management. The layout is built using Flexbox and media queries, enabling three completely different breakpoints with rearranged section order and sizes.
DETAILS.CHALLENGES: The project was great practice for responsive layout and working with Bootstrap 5. During adaptation, tasks arose around section order and placement on different screens, which were solved using `order`, `flex`, and custom SCSS breakpoints.

MOVIE_SEARCH.TITLE: Movie Search
MOVIE_SEARCH.TYPE_DESCRIPTION: A single-page application where all content is loaded without page reloads.
MOVIE_SEARCH.DESCRIPTION_SHORT: Movie search SPA using OMDb API, dynamic DOM rendering, and responsive SCSS styling.

DETAILS.ABOUT_PROJECT: A SPA application for searching movies by title using the OMDb API. The interface is dynamically generated using JavaScript without HTML templates.
DETAILS.OVERVIEW_PROJECT: The user enters a movie title, and the app sends a request to the OMDb API. Results are displayed as poster cards with the ability to load more results. Search is optimized using a debounce function. The interface is responsive and styled with pure SCSS, fully generated with JavaScript.
DETAILS.SPECIAL: The entire DOM is generated with JavaScript, including structure, styles, and result cards. The project follows a modular approach: logic is split into functions, each responsible for a specific part of the app. There is no pre-written HTML — everything is built via script.
DETAILS.CHALLENGES: The project was a practice in building SPAs without frameworks or templates. DOM manipulation was done from scratch, with implementation of debounce logic and basic architecture using JS modules. Pure SCSS was used for quick responsive styling of cards.

CRUD_BOOTSTRAP.TITLE: CRUD with Bootstrap
CRUD_BOOTSTRAP.TYPE_DESCRIPTION: An application with create, read, update, and delete operations.
CRUD_BOOTSTRAP.DESCRIPTION_SHORT: Angular CRUD app for user management with Bootstrap 5, Reactive Forms, and Firestore integration.

DETAILS.ABOUT_PROJECT: A demo application for managing a list of users with full CRUD operations. Built with Angular using Bootstrap 5 and Firebase Firestore as the data store.
DETAILS.OVERVIEW_PROJECT: The application allows creating, editing, deleting, and viewing users. All data is synchronized with Firebase Firestore. Forms are implemented using `Reactive Forms` with built-in and custom validation. The interface is responsive and built with Bootstrap components.
DETAILS.SPECIAL: The project uses Bootstrap 5 without customization. Special attention was paid to quality form validation: in addition to standard checks, custom pipes were implemented for dynamic value display. The interface is responsive and works well on all devices.
DETAILS.CHALLENGES: This was the first project involving CRUD apps with Angular and Firebase. Custom pipes for data formatting were implemented, along with full use of `Reactive Forms`. Thanks to stable Firestore and Bootstrap integration, the project was developed without technical difficulties.

CURRENCY_CONVERTER.TITLE: Currency Converter
CURRENCY_CONVERTER.TYPE_DESCRIPTION: A single-page application where all content is loaded without page reloads.
CURRENCY_CONVERTER.DESCRIPTION_SHORT: Minimalist Angular currency converter with Tailwind CSS, signals, and real-time NBU exchange rates.

DETAILS.ABOUT_PROJECT: An application for converting currencies using up-to-date rates from the National Bank of Ukraine. It allows quick conversion between selected currencies based on the official API.
DETAILS.OVERVIEW_PROJECT: The user selects a base and target currency, enters an amount — and gets the result based on the current NBU rate. Rates are fetched dynamically through a REST API. The interface is minimalist and responsive, built with Tailwind CSS.
DETAILS.SPECIAL: The project is built from scratch in Angular using `signals` for reactive state management. The interface is styled using Tailwind CSS, allowing fast and flexible styling without excess code.
DETAILS.CHALLENGES: This project was the first experience using `signals` in Angular. It also provided practice integrating with an external API without relying on third-party libraries for data handling.

PORTFOLIO.DESCRIPTION: Animated SPA portfolio focused on UI/UX skills. Includes synchronized slider, navigation and description blocks, custom animations, JSON typing effect, responsive layout, and multilingual support via ngx-translate.

WOT_BLITZ_STATISTICS.DESCRIPTION: An application for analyzing WoT Blitz statistics. Built using Angular, Firebase, and the Signals API. It features complex calculations based on players' battle stats.

WEATHER_FORECAST.DESCRIPTION: A weather forecast app displaying current conditions, hourly updates, and a 5-day outlook. Supports geolocation and manual city input.

JS_SLIDER.DESCRIPTION: A custom JavaScript slider with auto-scroll, button controls, indicators, keyboard and touch support. Dynamically builds the DOM and is easily configured. Supports both basic and mobile versions without jQuery.

GRID_TEMPLATE.DESCRIPTION: A template showcasing CSS Grid Layout capabilities with a focus on responsive design. Supports desktop and mobile devices and includes basic interactive elements using jQuery.

CRUD_MATERIAL.DESCRIPTION: Modern Angular 20 CRUD app for managing an electronics catalog with Angular Material and Firebase Firestore. Includes role-based access (anonymous user/admin), reactive forms, filtering, sorting, and pagination. Supports public demo data and personal sandbox records.

KINOZAL.DESCRIPTION: A responsive cinema website template built with Bootstrap 5.3.3. Uses a flexible flexbox grid, authorization and search forms, and basic user interaction logic. Great as a starting point for movie-related projects.

MOVIE_SEARCH.DESCRIPTION: A web application for searching movies via the OMDb API. Users can enter a movie title and get detailed information. Features dynamic DOM building, error handling, and responsive layout.

CRUD_BOOTSTRAP.DESCRIPTION: A CRUD application for managing a customer database using Firebase Realtime Database. Features adding, editing, and deleting data via reactive forms with custom validation. The interface is built with Bootstrap and is fully responsive.

CURRENCY_CONVERTER.DESCRIPTION: An app for currency conversion using up-to-date exchange rates. Allows users to quickly calculate exchange amounts. Features a responsive interface built with Tailwind CSS.

SKILLS.TITLE: My stack and approaches
SKILLS.SKILLS_BLOCK.TITLE: Skills and projects where I applied them:
SKILLS.APPROACH_BLOCK.TITLE: Tools:

GITHUB.TITLE: GitHub
GITHUB.DESCRIPTION: GitHub is my main tool for version control. I work with feature branches, write meaningful commits, and keep the project history clean.

FIREBASE.TITLE: Firebase
FIREBASE.DESCRIPTION: I use Firebase for hosting, database, and authentication. It’s perfect for quickly launching full-fledged projects.

GULP.TITLE: Gulp
GULP.DESCRIPTION: I use Gulp to automate project builds: minification, SCSS compilation, image optimization, and other tasks.

MATERIAL_UI.TITLE: Angular Material
MATERIAL_UI.DESCRIPTION: I use ready-made Angular Material components to build clear and structured interfaces in Angular projects.

BEM.TITLE: BEM
BEM.DESCRIPTION: BEM (Block, Element, Modifier) is the methodology I follow when writing styles to ensure maintainability and scalability of CSS.

NODE_JS.TITLE: Node.js
NODE_JS.DESCRIPTION: Node.js is the foundation of my frontend development workflow. I use it to run dev servers, manage project builds, and install dependencies.

WEBPACK.TITLE: Webpack
WEBPACK.DESCRIPTION: I use Webpack to bundle frontend projects. I configure module bundling, minification, and resource optimization.

WEBSTORM.TITLE: WebStorm
WEBSTORM.DESCRIPTION: WebStorm is my primary IDE for frontend development. I use its built-in tools for working with Git, debugging, and code refactoring.

DRY.TITLE: DRY
DRY.DESCRIPTION: DRY (Don’t Repeat Yourself) — I always strive to avoid code duplication by extracting reusable parts into separate functions and components.

KISS.TITLE: KISS
KISS.DESCRIPTION: KISS (Keep It Simple, Stupid) — I aim to write code that’s as simple and clear as possible, avoiding unnecessary complexity and abstractions.

SOLID.TITLE: SOLID
SOLID.DESCRIPTION: I apply SOLID principles to design scalable and maintainable code, especially in Angular components and services.

YAGNI.TITLE: YAGNI
YAGNI.DESCRIPTION: YAGNI (You Aren't Gonna Need It) — I avoid unnecessary functionality and implement only the features the project truly needs.

DESCRIPTION.BUILT_WITH: Crafted using Angular, Tailwind CSS and Firebase.
